name: document lifecycle rejects illegal transition (record cannot be revoked)

source.purpose: Lock the per-type lifecycle contract: type=record can transition to void only — never revoked, superseded, or resolved. Without this gate, the Archivist can slide a record into a status that doesn't apply, leaking through the closed-type taxonomy.

doc_intake_log.title: Intake hall ledger entry — week 14
doc_intake_log.retrievalCue: Ledger record of who passed through the hall.
doc_intake_log.authorizes: attests passage of three subjects through intake on day 14
doc_intake_log.originalSummary: Three subjects logged: Maret, Kael, Dav — all stamped 'cleared'.
doc_intake_log.currentSummary: Three subjects logged: Maret, Kael, Dav — all stamped 'cleared'.

input.playerInput: You insist the entries be revoked.

narrator.prose: The clerk shakes his head. "You can't revoke a ledger. Records are corrected, not cancelled."
